lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY lifecycle for the 3 uncovered configinspection WRITE ops: POST /v1/configinspection/diagnosis/save (create a diagnosis object), POST /v1/configinspection/diagnosis/request (run a diagnosis), DELETE /v1/configinspection/diagnosis/terminate/{diagnosis_id} (terminate). Config Inspection diagnoses a CLOUD ACCOUNT (csp_type SCP or a foreign CSP) and the request/save bodies need REAL credentials of the account being inspected (diagnosisrequest: access_key/secret_key/tenant_id; diagnosisobjectrequest: a nested auth_key_request with a real auth_key_id, plus diagnosis_account_id). We do NOT have a throwaway inspectable account + auth key, so every write is a GUARDED OPTIONAL call with doc-sample ids and broad expect_status: it CALLS the endpoint (recording coverage for the 3 catalog keys) and 4xx's as expected. Bodies/field rules from data/api_docs.json (diagnosisobjectrequest, diagnosisrequest, diagnosisschedulerequest, authkeyrequest) — _UNPROVEN. save returns FLAT $.diagnosis_id (configinspectioncreateresponse) — soft-captured so that, if save unexpectedly succeeds, probe-reads can exercise GET .../diagnosis/detail/{diagnosis_id} and the terminate DELETE targets the real id; otherwise terminate fires against the doc-sample {diagnosis_id} (still records the DELETE write, 4xx). diagnosis_name pattern ^[a-zA-Z0-9-_]+$. Each write is isolated in its own group so a 4xx is a warning, not a run failure; nothing real is created so teardown is the terminate call itself (no separate cleanup resource).
